quick_facts.label: Core role
quick_facts.value: A Nursing Tutor (also termed a Sister Tutor or, in some services, Tutor/Clinical Instructor) organizes and delivers nursing teaching, plans students’ practical and ward experience, and provides clinical teaching and supervision.

quick_facts.label: Qualification context
quick_facts.value: For an Indian Nursing Council (INC) College of Nursing tutor post, M.Sc. Nursing is stated as preferable; B.Sc. Nursing or Post Basic B.Sc. Nursing with one year of experience is also listed. Requirements differ by programme and recruiting employer.

quick_facts.label: Registration context
quick_facts.value: INC minimum-requirement guidance says nursing teachers must hold a basic university/equivalent qualification recognized under the INC Act and be registered under the applicable State Nursing Registration Act.

quick_facts.label: M.Sc. Nursing route
quick_facts.value: INC lists M.Sc. Nursing admission prerequisites including registration as a nurse and midwife/equivalent with a State Nursing Registration Council, a B.Sc./B.Sc. (Hons.)/Post Basic B.Sc. Nursing qualification with at least 55% aggregate marks, and one year of relevant work experience under the applicable basic or post-basic route.

quick_facts.label: Teaching and clinical workload
quick_facts.value: INC guidance states that nursing teachers other than the Principal and Vice-Principal should spend at least four hours daily in the clinical area for clinical teaching and/or supervision of care.

sections.heading: Teaching, student support, and clinical supervision
paragraphs: The role combines classroom teaching with supervised practice. The College of Nursing at VMMC & Safdarjung Hospital describes the Sister Tutor as responsible to the Principal for organizing and conducting the teaching programme. Listed work includes planning orientation and teaching programmes, preparing classes and demonstrations, teaching nursing subjects, arranging practical experience and ward assignments, guiding students’ study and use of the library, and giving individual attention where needed.

INC’s college standards also place nursing faculty in clinical teaching: except for the Principal and Vice-Principal, teachers should spend at least four hours each day in the clinical area for teaching and/or supervision of care. This makes safe clinical instruction, learner feedback, coordination with clinical areas, and record-ready academic planning central to the role.
bullets: Plan lessons, orientations, demonstrations, practical placements, ward teaching, and student schedules.
Teach nursing subjects and support students with assignments, study methods, reference use, and individual learning needs.
Supervise or teach in clinical areas and coordinate practical learning with hospitals or other practice settings.
Work under the academic leadership of the Principal/Vice-Principal and within the institution’s INC-related faculty standards.

sections.heading: Entry routes from M.Sc. Nursing and other nursing qualifications
paragraphs: For the canonical M.Sc. Nursing pathway, INC’s published eligibility criteria for M.Sc. Nursing require State Nursing Registration Council registration as a nurse and midwife or equivalent, an eligible B.Sc. Nursing-family degree with at least 55% aggregate marks, and one year of work experience as specified for the basic or post-basic route.

An M.Sc. Nursing is advantageous but is not a universal minimum for every role called “Tutor.” INC’s College of Nursing minimum requirements describe M.Sc. Nursing as preferable for a tutor; they also permit B.Sc. Nursing or Post Basic B.Sc. Nursing with one year of experience. By contrast, a 2023 NEIGRIHMS draft recruitment-rule document proposed M.Sc. Nursing from a recognized institution/university as the essential direct-recruitment qualification. Candidates should therefore read the current notification of the specific college, university, hospital, state service commission, or central institution before applying.

For GNM teaching faculty, INC guidance separately lists tutor eligibility that can include M.Sc. Nursing, B.Sc. Nursing (Basic/Post Basic), or Diploma in Nursing Education and Administration, with two years of professional experience. This programme-specific distinction is important when comparing vacancies.
bullets: Complete an INC-recognized nursing qualification and maintain the registration required by the relevant State Nursing Registration Act.
For the M.Sc. Nursing route, meet the applicable INC academic, registration, and experience prerequisites for admission.
Build clinical and/or teaching experience, since experience requirements differ across college standards, programme regulations, and employer recruitment rules.
Check the vacancy’s exact designation: “Nursing Tutor,” “Sister Tutor,” and “Tutor/Clinical Instructor” may have different recruitment rules.

sections.heading: Skills evidenced by the work
paragraphs: The formal duty descriptions point to a practical skill mix: lesson and placement planning, classroom and demonstration teaching, assessment-oriented course planning, learner guidance, use of educational materials and references, and clinical supervision. Because tutors work across academic and care settings, they also need clear communication and coordination with academic leaders and clinical teams.

Applicants should be able to show both nursing competence and educational practice rather than treating the role as classroom-only work. INC’s expectation of daily clinical teaching/supervision for most nursing teachers reinforces the importance of current clinical judgment and the ability to connect theory to safe patient care.
bullets: Instructional planning and delivery, including demonstrations and orientation.
Clinical teaching, supervision of care, and coordination of practical or ward assignments.
Student mentoring, feedback, individual academic support, and guidance on study resources.
Communication and collaboration with the Principal/Vice-Principal, nursing faculty, and clinical-service teams.

sections.heading: Where Nursing Tutors work
paragraphs: The core workplace is a School or College of Nursing, with regular work extending into affiliated hospital wards and other clinical learning areas. VMMC & Safdarjung Hospital’s official description situates the Sister Tutor in the College/School of Nursing while assigning responsibility for students’ practical experience and ward teaching. INC standards similarly require substantial clinical-area teaching or supervision for most nursing teachers.

Public-sector nursing education services may use related designations. Maharashtra’s State Nursing Cell lists “Nursing Officers/Tutors/Clinical Instructors” among its public-health nursing cadres, while NEIGRIHMS uses the designation “Tutor/Clinical Instructor” in its recruitment-rule material.
bullets: Nursing colleges and schools.
Teaching hospitals and affiliated clinical wards.
Government health-service nursing-training establishments.
Institutions that use related titles such as Sister Tutor or Tutor/Clinical Instructor.

sections.heading: Academic progression
paragraphs: In INC College of Nursing minimum requirements, the teaching hierarchy above Tutor includes Assistant Professor, Associate Professor, Professor, Vice-Principal-cum-Professor, and Principal-cum-Professor. The listed experience thresholds increase at each stage: Assistant Professor requires M.Sc. Nursing with three years of teaching experience; Associate Professor requires a total of eight years’ experience including five years’ teaching experience; and Professor requires 12 years’ experience with 10 years’ teaching experience after M.Sc. Nursing. These are regulatory staffing standards, not an automatic promotion entitlement.

An M.Sc. Nursing therefore supports progression into higher academic posts, but candidates should verify the applicable university statutes, state recruitment rules, and employer-specific selection criteria for each vacancy.
bullets: Tutor → Assistant Professor, subject to the stated M.Sc. Nursing and experience requirements.
Assistant Professor → Associate Professor → Professor, with progressively higher total and teaching-experience thresholds in INC college standards.
Senior academic leadership roles include Vice-Principal-cum-Professor and Principal-cum-Professor.

sections.heading: Compensation context: employer and state rules matter
paragraphs: Pay is highly employer-, state-, cadre-, and appointment-rule-specific, so it should not be treated as a national salary benchmark. For dated public-sector context, a Kerala Directorate of Health Services appointment order dated September 7, 2024 appointed Nursing Tutors in the pay scale of ₹50,200–₹105,300. A NEIGRIHMS document concerning amended draft recruitment rules dated 2023 proposed Level 10 of the pay matrix for Tutor; it is a draft-rule reference rather than evidence of a universal pay entitlement.

Applicants should use the pay level or scale printed in the live recruitment notification and confirm whether the post is permanent, temporary, contractual, or governed by a particular state or central pay framework.
bullets: Example only: Kerala Health Services appointment order dated September 7, 2024 — ₹50,200–₹105,300 for Nursing Tutor appointments covered by that order.
Example only: NEIGRIHMS 2023 amended draft rules proposed Pay Matrix Level 10 for Tutor.
Do not extrapolate either example to private colleges, other states, or later recruitment cycles.
